content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to demonstrate how a pointer can be decremented to move to the previous memory location. You will be given an array of integers and an index. You need to print the value at the given index and the value at the index before it using pointer decrement.


Input Format

The first line contains the size of the array `n`.
The second line contains `n` space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.
The third line contains an integer `index`.


Output Format

The first line should print the value at the given `index`.
The second line should print the value at the `index - 1`.
constraints: Array elements fit in a 32‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 15), the size of the array. Second line contains n space‑separated 32‑bit signed integers. Third line contains an integer index (1‑based, 1 ≤ index ≤ n).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the value at the given index on one line. If a previous element exists (index > 1), print the value at index‑1 on the next line. Do not print extra blank lines between test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Array is [10,20,30,40,50]; index=3 (1‑based). Element at position 3 is 30; previous element (position 2) is 20.
Array [1,2,3]; index=2 gives element 2 and the previous element 1.
Only one element exists. The value at index 1 is 42. No previous element, so only one line is printed.

solution.approach: The task demonstrates pointer arithmetic. For each test case we read the array, obtain a pointer to the element at position index‑1 (0‑based) and output the dereferenced value. If index>1 we decrement the pointer (ptr--) to move to the previous memory location and output that value. This directly mirrors how pointers work in C/C++. The algorithm runs in O(n) time per test case for reading the array and O(1) for answering the query, which easily satisfies the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case (reading the array), O(1) for the queries. Total O(∑n) ≤ O(150).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) per test case to store the array (max 15 integers).

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        index = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(arr[index - 1]))
        if index > 1:
            out.append(str(arr[index - 2]))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
